Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal - The Nation Newspaper Monday, March 16, 2026 Advertise With Us E-paper. March 16, 2026 by  Chikodi Okereocha. # Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal. Manufacturers have welcomed Chinese President Xi Jinping’s decision to remove tariffs on imports from 53 African countries starting May 1, 2026, noting that the zero-tariff policy will open one of the world’s largest consumer markets to African producers and manufacturers. In the short term, resource-rich countries may see faster growth in shipments of oil, minerals, and agricultural commodities. “The zero-tariff window should serve as a catalyst for industrial upgrading, regional value chains, and stronger export competitiveness,” it said. Instead, these gains will depend on Africa’s ability to move up the value chain, producing semi-processed and finished goods that meet international quality standards. * ‘What China’s Xi told me about Taiwan’. * What Nigeria can learn from the China’s poverty eradication model. ##### Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal 1 minute ago. https://thenationonlineng.net/nigerian-manufacturers-others-to-save-1-4b-yearly-on-chinese-tariff-removal/amp/
